14#ifndef NATS_H_
15#define NATS_H_

17#ifdef __cplusplus
18extern "C" {
19#endif

21#include <stdlib.h>
22#include <stdint.h>
23#include <stdbool.h>
24#include <inttypes.h>
25#include <stdio.h>

27#include "status.h"
28#include "version.h"

37#if defined(_WIN32)
38 #include <winsock2.h>
39 #if defined(nats_EXPORTS)
40 #define NATS_EXTERN __declspec(dllexport)
41 #elif defined(nats_IMPORTS)
42 #define NATS_EXTERN __declspec(dllimport)
43 #else
44 #define NATS_EXTERN
45 #endif
46
47 typedef SOCKET natsSock;
48#else
49 #define NATS_EXTERN
50 typedef int natsSock;
51#endif

86#define NATS_DEFAULT_URL "nats://localhost:4222"
87
97 #define JSMsgSize "Nats-Msg-Size"
98
111 #define JSMsgRollup "Nats-Rollup"
112
120 #define JSMsgRollupSubject "sub"
121
129 #define JSMsgRollupAll "all"
130
131 // Headers for republished messages and direct get.
132 #define JSStream "Nats-Stream"
133 #define JSSequence "Nats-Sequence"
134 #define JSLastSequence "Nats-Last-Sequence"
135 #define JSTimeStamp "Nats-Time-Stamp"
136 #define JSSubject "Nats-Subject"
137
138//
139// Types.
140//
152typedef struct __natsConnection natsConnection;
153
159typedef struct __natsStatistics natsStatistics;
160
165typedef struct __natsSubscription natsSubscription;
166
172typedef struct __natsMsg natsMsg;
173
178typedef struct __natsOptions natsOptions;
179
187typedef char natsInbox;
188
193typedef struct __natsHeader natsHeader;
194
200typedef struct __natsClientConfig
201{
202 int64_t DefaultWriteDeadline;
203
204 int64_t LockSpinCount;
205
206 // Subscription message delivery thread control
207 bool DefaultToThreadPool;
208 int ThreadPoolMax;
209
210 // Reply message delivery thread control
211 bool DefaultRepliesToThreadPool;
212 bool UseSeparatePoolForReplies;
213 int ReplyThreadPoolMax;
214} natsClientConfig;
215
239typedef struct natsMsgList
240{
241 natsMsg **Msgs;
242 int Count;
243
244} natsMsgList;

250typedef struct natsMetadata
251{
255 const char **List;
259 int Count;
260
261} natsMetadata;

271typedef struct __jsCtx jsCtx;
272
288typedef struct jsPubOptions
289{
290 int64_t MaxWait;
291 const char *MsgId;
292 const char *ExpectStream;
293 const char *ExpectLastMsgId;
294 uint64_t ExpectLastSeq;
295 uint64_t ExpectLastSubjectSeq;
296 bool ExpectNoMessage;
297 int64_t MsgTTL;
298
299} jsPubOptions;
300
304typedef enum
305{
306 js_LimitsPolicy = 0,
307 js_InterestPolicy,
308 js_WorkQueuePolicy,
309
310} jsRetentionPolicy;
311
315typedef enum
316{
317 js_DiscardOld = 0,
318 js_DiscardNew,
319
320} jsDiscardPolicy;
321
325typedef enum
326{
327 js_FileStorage = 0,
328 js_MemoryStorage,
329
330} jsStorageType;
331
335typedef enum
336{
337 js_StorageCompressionNone = 0,
338 js_StorageCompressionS2,
339} jsStorageCompression;
340
344typedef enum
345{
346 js_DeliverAll = 0,
347 js_DeliverLast,
348 js_DeliverNew,
349 js_DeliverByStartSequence,
350 js_DeliverByStartTime,
351 js_DeliverLastPerSubject,
352
353} jsDeliverPolicy;
354
358typedef enum
359{
360 js_AckExplicit = 0,
361 js_AckNone,
362 js_AckAll,
363
364
365} jsAckPolicy;
366
370typedef enum
371{
372 js_ReplayInstant = 0,
373 js_ReplayOriginal,
374
375} jsReplayPolicy;
376
377
389typedef struct jsPlacement
390{
391 const char *Cluster;
392 const char **Tags;
393 int TagsLen;
394
395} jsPlacement;
396
406typedef struct jsExternalStream
407{
408 const char *APIPrefix;
409 const char *DeliverPrefix;
410
411} jsExternalStream;
412
425typedef struct jsStreamSource
426{
427 const char *Name;
428 uint64_t OptStartSeq;
429 int64_t OptStartTime;
430 const char *FilterSubject;
431 jsExternalStream *External;
432 // Domain and External are mutually exclusive.
433 // If Domain is set, an External value will be created with
434 // the APIPrefix constructed based on the Domain value.
435 const char *Domain;
436
437} jsStreamSource;
438
442typedef struct jsRePublish
443{
444 const char *Source;
445 const char *Destination;
446 bool HeadersOnly;
447
448} jsRePublish;
449
454typedef struct jsSubjectTransformConfig
455{
456 const char *Source;
457 const char *Destination;
458} jsSubjectTransformConfig;
459
464typedef struct jsStreamConsumerLimits
465{
466 int64_t InactiveThreshold;
467 int MaxAckPending;
468} jsStreamConsumerLimits;
469
549typedef struct jsStreamConfig {
550 const char *Name;
551 const char *Description;
552 const char **Subjects;
553 int SubjectsLen;
554 jsRetentionPolicy Retention;
555 int64_t MaxConsumers;
556 int64_t MaxMsgs;
557 int64_t MaxBytes;
558 int64_t MaxAge;
559 int64_t MaxMsgsPerSubject;
560 int32_t MaxMsgSize;
561 jsDiscardPolicy Discard;
562 jsStorageType Storage;
563 int64_t Replicas;
564 bool NoAck;
565 const char *Template;
566 int64_t Duplicates;
567 jsPlacement *Placement;
568 jsStreamSource *Mirror;
569 jsStreamSource **Sources;
570 int SourcesLen;
571 bool Sealed;
572 bool DenyDelete;
573 bool DenyPurge;
574
577 bool AllowRollup;
578
581 jsRePublish *RePublish;
582
585 bool AllowDirect;
586
589 bool MirrorDirect;
590
593 bool DiscardNewPerSubject;
594
598 natsMetadata Metadata;
599
602 jsStorageCompression Compression;
603
605 uint64_t FirstSeq;
606
609 jsSubjectTransformConfig SubjectTransform;
610
613 jsStreamConsumerLimits ConsumerLimits;
614
617 bool AllowMsgTTL;
618
622 int64_t SubjectDeleteMarkerTTL;
623
624} jsStreamConfig;
625
629typedef struct jsLostStreamData
630{
631 uint64_t *Msgs;
632 int MsgsLen;
633 uint64_t Bytes;
634
635} jsLostStreamData;
636
642typedef struct jsStreamStateSubject
643{
644 const char *Subject;
645 uint64_t Msgs;
646
647} jsStreamStateSubject;
648
678typedef struct jsStreamStateSubjects
679{
680 jsStreamStateSubject *List;
681 int Count;
682
683} jsStreamStateSubjects;
684
691typedef struct jsStreamState
692{
693 uint64_t Msgs;
694 uint64_t Bytes;
695 uint64_t FirstSeq;
696 int64_t FirstTime;
697 uint64_t LastSeq;
698 int64_t LastTime;
699 int64_t NumSubjects;
700 jsStreamStateSubjects *Subjects;
701 uint64_t NumDeleted;
702 uint64_t *Deleted;
703 int DeletedLen;
704 jsLostStreamData *Lost;
705 int64_t Consumers;
706
707} jsStreamState;
708
713typedef struct jsPeerInfo
714{
715 char *Name;
716 bool Current;
717 bool Offline;
718 int64_t Active;
719 uint64_t Lag;
720
721} jsPeerInfo;
722
727typedef struct jsClusterInfo
728{
729 char *Name;
730 char *Leader;
731 jsPeerInfo **Replicas;
732 int ReplicasLen;
733
734} jsClusterInfo;
735
739typedef struct jsStreamSourceInfo
740{
741 char *Name;
742 jsExternalStream *External;
743 uint64_t Lag;
744 int64_t Active;
745 const char * FilterSubject;
746 jsSubjectTransformConfig *SubjectTransforms;
747 int SubjectTransformsLen;
748
749} jsStreamSourceInfo;
750
754typedef struct jsStreamAlternate
755{
756 const char *Name;
757 const char *Domain;
758 const char *Cluster;
759
760} jsStreamAlternate;
761
768typedef struct jsStreamInfo
769{
770 jsStreamConfig *Config;
771 int64_t Created;
772 jsStreamState State;
773 jsClusterInfo *Cluster;
774 jsStreamSourceInfo *Mirror;
775 jsStreamSourceInfo **Sources;
776 int SourcesLen;
777 jsStreamAlternate **Alternates;
778 int AlternatesLen;
779
780} jsStreamInfo;
781
789typedef struct jsStreamInfoList
790{
791 jsStreamInfo **List;
792 int Count;
793
794} jsStreamInfoList;
795
803typedef struct jsStreamNamesList
804{
805 char **List;
806 int Count;
807
808} jsStreamNamesList;
809
853typedef struct jsConsumerConfig
854{
855 const char *Name;
856 const char *Durable;
857 const char *Description;
858 jsDeliverPolicy DeliverPolicy;
859 uint64_t OptStartSeq;
860 int64_t OptStartTime;
861 jsAckPolicy AckPolicy;
862 int64_t AckWait;
863 int64_t MaxDeliver;
864 int64_t *BackOff;
865 int BackOffLen;
866 const char *FilterSubject;
867 jsReplayPolicy ReplayPolicy;
868 uint64_t RateLimit;
869 const char *SampleFrequency;
870 int64_t MaxWaiting;
871 int64_t MaxAckPending;
872 bool FlowControl;
873 int64_t Heartbeat;
874 bool HeadersOnly;
875
876 // Pull based options.
877 int64_t MaxRequestBatch;
878 int64_t MaxRequestExpires;
879 int64_t MaxRequestMaxBytes;
880
881 // Push based options.
882 const char *DeliverSubject;
883 const char *DeliverGroup;
884
885 // Ephemeral inactivity threshold.
886 int64_t InactiveThreshold;
887
888 // Generally inherited by parent stream and other markers, now can be configured directly.
889 int64_t Replicas;
890 // Force memory storage.
891 bool MemoryStorage;
892
893 // Configuration options introduced in 2.10
894
895 const char **FilterSubjects;
896 int FilterSubjectsLen;
897 natsMetadata Metadata;
898
899 // Configuration options introduced in 2.11
900
904 int64_t PauseUntil;
905
909 const char *PriorityPolicy;
910
915 int64_t PinnedTTL;
916
918 const char **PriorityGroups;
919 int PriorityGroupsLen;
920} jsConsumerConfig;
921
939typedef struct jsConsumerSequenceMismatch
940{
941 uint64_t Stream;
942 uint64_t ConsumerClient;
943 uint64_t ConsumerServer;
944
945} jsConsumerSequenceMismatch;

963{
970 const char *Stream;
980 const char *Consumer;
995 const char *Queue;
1008 bool ManualAck;
1013 jsConsumerConfig Config;
1020 bool Ordered;
1021
1022} jsSubOptions;

1027typedef struct jsSequencePair
1028{
1029 uint64_t Consumer;
1030 uint64_t Stream;
1031
1032} jsSequencePair;

1037typedef struct jsSequenceInfo
1038{
1039 uint64_t Consumer;
1040 uint64_t Stream;
1041 int64_t Last;
1042
1043} jsSequenceInfo;

1049 {

1051 char *PinnedClientID;
1052 int64_t PinnedTS;
1053 } jsPriorityGroupState;

1061typedef struct jsConsumerInfo
1062{
1063 char *Stream;
1064 char *Name;
1065 int64_t Created;
1066 jsConsumerConfig *Config;
1067 jsSequenceInfo Delivered;
1068 jsSequenceInfo AckFloor;
1069 int64_t NumAckPending;
1070 int64_t NumRedelivered;
1071 int64_t NumWaiting;
1072 uint64_t NumPending;
1073 jsClusterInfo *Cluster;
1074 bool PushBound;
1075 bool Paused;
1076 int64_t PauseRemaining;
1077 jsPriorityGroupState *PriorityGroups;
1078 int PriorityGroupsLen;
1079} jsConsumerInfo;

1089{
1090 jsConsumerInfo **List;
1091 int Count;
1092
1093} jsConsumerInfoList;

1103{
1104 char **List;
1105 int Count;
1106
1107} jsConsumerNamesList;

1115{

1118 int64_t PauseRemaining;
1119} jsConsumerPauseResponse;

1124typedef struct jsAPIStats
1125{
1126 uint64_t Total;
1127 uint64_t Errors;
1128
1129} jsAPIStats;

1134typedef struct jsAccountLimits
1135{
1136 int64_t MaxMemory;
1137 int64_t MaxStore;
1138 int64_t MaxStreams;
1139 int64_t MaxConsumers;
1140 int64_t MaxAckPending;
1141 int64_t MemoryMaxStreamBytes;
1142 int64_t StoreMaxStreamBytes;
1143 bool MaxBytesRequired;
1144
1145} jsAccountLimits;
1146
1147typedef struct jsTier
1148{
1149 const char *Name;
1150 uint64_t Memory;
1151 uint64_t Store;
1152 int64_t Streams;
1153 int64_t Consumers;
1154 jsAccountLimits Limits;
1155
1156} jsTier;
1157
1161typedef struct jsAccountInfo
1162{
1163 uint64_t Memory;
1164 uint64_t Store;
1165 int64_t Streams;
1166 int64_t Consumers;
1167 char *Domain;
1168 jsAPIStats API;
1169 jsAccountLimits Limits;
1170 jsTier **Tiers;
1171 int TiersLen;
1172
1173} jsAccountInfo;
1174
1182typedef struct jsMsgMetaData
1183{
1184 jsSequencePair Sequence;
1185 uint64_t NumDelivered;
1186 uint64_t NumPending;
1187 int64_t Timestamp;
1188 char *Stream;
1189 char *Consumer;
1190 char *Domain;
1191
1192} jsMsgMetaData;
1193
1197typedef struct jsPubAck
1198{
1199 char *Stream;
1200 uint64_t Sequence;
1201 char *Domain;
1202 bool Duplicate;
1203
1204} jsPubAck;
1205
1210typedef struct jsPubAckErr
1211{
1212 natsMsg *Msg;
1213 natsStatus Err;
1214 jsErrCode ErrCode;
1215 const char *ErrText;
1216
1217} jsPubAckErr;
1218
1219#ifndef BUILD_IN_DOXYGEN
1220// Forward declarations
1221typedef void (*jsPubAckErrHandler)(jsCtx *js, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
1222typedef void (*jsPubAckHandler)(jsCtx *js, natsMsg *msg, jsPubAck *pa, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
1223#endif

1234typedef struct jsDirectGetMsgOptions
1235{
1236 uint64_t Sequence;
1237 const char *NextBySubject;
1238 const char *LastBySubject;
1239
1240} jsDirectGetMsgOptions;
1241
1247typedef struct jsFetchRequest
1248{
1249 int64_t Expires;
1250 int Batch;
1251 int64_t MaxBytes;
1252 bool NoWait;
1253 int64_t Heartbeat;
1254
1255 // The use of these fields require nats-server v2.11.0 or later
1256 int64_t MinPending;
1257 int64_t MinAckPending;
1258 const char *ID;
1260 const char *Group;
1261
1262} jsFetchRequest;
1263
1285typedef void (*jsFetchCompleteHandler)(natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *sub, natsStatus s, void *closure);
1286
1297typedef bool (*jsFetchNextHandler)(int *messages, int64_t *maxBytes, natsSubscription *sub, void *closure);
1298
1304typedef struct jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync
1305{
1306 int64_t Timeout;
1307 int MaxMessages;
1308 int64_t MaxBytes;
1309
1317 bool NoWait;
1318
1322 jsFetchCompleteHandler CompleteHandler;
1323 void *CompleteHandlerClosure;
1324
1327 int64_t Heartbeat;
1328
1333 const char *Group;
1334
1340 int64_t MinPending;
1341
1347 int64_t MinAckPending;
1348
1352 int FetchSize;
1353
1360 int KeepAhead;
1361
1367 jsFetchNextHandler NextHandler;
1368 void *NextHandlerClosure;
1369
1370} jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync;
1371
1377typedef struct jsOptionsPublishAsync
1378{
1379 int64_t MaxPending;
1380
1381 // If jsPubAckHandler is specified, the callback will be invoked
1382 // for every asynchronous published message, either as a positive
1383 // result, or with the error encountered when publishing that
1384 // message. If this callback is specified, ErrHandler (see below)
1385 // will be ignored.
1386 jsPubAckHandler AckHandler;
1387 void *AckHandlerClosure;
1388
1389 // This callback is invoked for messages published asynchronously
1390 // when an error is returned by the server or if the library has
1391 // timed-out waiting for an acknowledgment back from the server
1392 // (if publish uses the jsPubOptions.MaxWait).
1393 jsPubAckErrHandler ErrHandler;
1394 void *ErrHandlerClosure;
1395
1396 int64_t StallWait;
1397
1398} jsOptionsPublishAsync;
1399
1409typedef struct jsOptionsStreamPurge
1410{
1411 const char *Subject;
1412 uint64_t Sequence;
1413 uint64_t Keep;
1414
1415} jsOptionsStreamPurge;
1416
1420typedef struct jsOptionsStreamInfo
1421{
1422 bool DeletedDetails;
1423 const char *SubjectsFilter;
1424
1425} jsOptionsStreamInfo;
1426
1433typedef struct jsOptionsStream
1434{
1435 jsOptionsStreamPurge Purge;
1436 jsOptionsStreamInfo Info;
1437
1438} jsOptionsStream;
1439
1445typedef struct jsOptions
1446{
1447 const char *Prefix;
1448 const char *Domain;
1449 int64_t Wait;
1450 jsOptionsPublishAsync PublishAsync;
1451 jsOptionsPullSubscribeAsync PullSubscribeAsync;
1452 jsOptionsStream Stream;
1453
1454} jsOptions;
1455
1459typedef struct __kvStore kvStore;
1460
1464typedef struct __kvEntry kvEntry;
1465
1469typedef struct __kvStatus kvStatus;
1470
1474typedef struct __kvWatcher kvWatcher;
1475
1479typedef enum
1480{
1481 kvOp_Unknown = 0,
1482 kvOp_Put,
1483 kvOp_Delete,
1484 kvOp_Purge,
1485
1486} kvOperation;
1487
1493typedef struct kvConfig
1494{
1495 const char *Bucket;
1496 const char *Description;
1497 int32_t MaxValueSize;
1498 uint8_t History;
1499 int64_t TTL;
1500 int64_t MaxBytes;
1501 jsStorageType StorageType;
1502 int Replicas;
1503 jsRePublish *RePublish;
1504 jsStreamSource *Mirror;
1505 jsStreamSource **Sources;
1506 int SourcesLen;
1507
1508} kvConfig;
1509
1515typedef struct kvWatchOptions
1516{
1517 bool IgnoreDeletes;
1518 bool IncludeHistory;
1519 bool MetaOnly;
1520 int64_t Timeout;
1521 bool UpdatesOnly;
1522
1523} kvWatchOptions;
1524
1530typedef struct kvPurgeOptions
1531{
1532 // How long to wait (in milliseconds) for some operations to complete.
1533 int64_t Timeout;
1534
1535 // When calling kvStore_PurgeDeletes(), all keys that have a delete or
1536 // purge marker as the last entry are gathered and then those keys
1537 // are purged of their content, including the marker.
1538 // Starting with NATS C client v3.3.0, if this option is not specified,
1539 // only the markers older than 30 minutes will be deleted. Use this
1540 // option to set the limit or a negative value to force removal of
1541 // markers regardless of their age.
1542 // The value is expressed as a time in nanoseconds.
1543 int64_t DeleteMarkersOlderThan;
1544
1545} kvPurgeOptions;
1546
1564typedef struct kvEntryList
1565{
1566 kvEntry **Entries;
1567 int Count;
1568
1569} kvEntryList;
1570
1588typedef struct kvKeysList
1589{
1590 char **Keys;
1591 int Count;
1592
1593} kvKeysList;
1594
1598typedef struct __objStore objStore;
1599

1613
1619typedef struct __objStoreGet objStoreGet;
1620

1901typedef struct objStoreInfo
1902{

2011
2012#if defined(NATS_HAS_STREAMING)
2017typedef struct __stanConnection stanConnection;
2018
2023typedef struct __stanSubscription stanSubscription;
2024
2029typedef struct __stanMsg stanMsg;
2030
2035typedef struct __stanConnOptions stanConnOptions;
2036
2041typedef struct __stanSubOptions stanSubOptions;
2042#endif
2043
// end of typesGroup
2045
2046//
2047// Callbacks.
2048//
2049
2072typedef void (*natsMsgHandler)(
2073 natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *sub, natsMsg *msg, void *closure);
2074
2088typedef void (*natsConnectionHandler)(
2089 natsConnection *nc, void *closure);
2090
2102typedef natsStatus (*natsProxyConnHandler)(
2103 natsSock *fd, char *host, int port, void *closure);
2104
2111typedef void (*natsErrHandler)(
2112 natsConnection *nc, natsSubscription *subscription, natsStatus err,
2113 void *closure);
2114
2128typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_Attach)(
2129 void **userData,
2130 void *loop,
2131 natsConnection *nc,
2132 natsSock socket);
2133
2143typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_ReadAddRemove)(
2144 void *userData,
2145 bool add);
2146
2156typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_WriteAddRemove)(
2157 void *userData,
2158 bool add);
2159
2167typedef natsStatus (*natsEvLoop_Detach)(
2168 void *userData);
2169
2191typedef natsStatus (*natsUserJWTHandler)(
2192 char **userJWT,
2193 char **customErrTxt,
2194 void *closure);
2195
2196
2222typedef natsStatus (*natsSignatureHandler)(
2223 char **customErrTxt,
2224 unsigned char **signature,
2225 int *signatureLength,
2226 const char *nonce,
2227 void *closure);
2228
2237typedef const char* (*natsTokenHandler)(void *closure);
2238
2239
2247typedef void (*natsOnCompleteCB)(void *closure);
2248
2269typedef int64_t (*natsCustomReconnectDelayHandler)(natsConnection *nc, int attempts, void *closure);
2270
2294typedef int (*natsSSLVerifyCb)(int preverifyOk, void *ctx);
2295
2296#ifdef BUILD_IN_DOXYGEN
2330typedef void (*jsPubAckErrHandler)(jsCtx *js, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
2331
2373typedef void (*jsPubAckHandler)(jsCtx *js, natsMsg *msg, jsPubAck *pa, jsPubAckErr *pae, void *closure);
2374#endif
2375
2376#if defined(NATS_HAS_STREAMING)
2385typedef void (*stanPubAckHandler)(const char *guid, const char *error, void *closure);
2386
2396typedef void (*stanMsgHandler)(
2397 stanConnection *sc, stanSubscription *sub, const char *channel, stanMsg *msg, void *closure);
2398
2405typedef void (*stanConnectionLostHandler)(
2406 stanConnection *sc, const char* errorTxt, void *closure);
2407#endif
2408
// end of callbacksGroup

2860/*
2861 * The above is for doxygen. The proper syntax for username/password
2862 * is without the '\' character:
2863 *
2864 * nats://localhost:4222
2865 * nats://user@localhost:4222
2866 * nats://user:password@localhost:4222
2867 */

9343//
9344// Microservices.
9345//
9346
9389typedef struct micro_client_s microClient;
9390
9394typedef struct __for_forward_compatibility_only microClientConfig;
9395
9405typedef struct micro_endpoint_s microEndpoint;
9406
9414typedef struct micro_endpoint_config_s microEndpointConfig;
9415
9425typedef struct micro_endpoint_info_s microEndpointInfo;
9426
9435typedef struct micro_endpoint_stats_s microEndpointStats;
9436
9449typedef struct micro_error_s microError;
9450
9458typedef struct micro_group_s microGroup;
9459
9466typedef struct micro_group_config_s microGroupConfig;
9467
9473typedef struct micro_request_s microRequest;
9474
9486typedef struct micro_service_s microService;
9487
9497typedef struct micro_service_config_s microServiceConfig;
9498
9508typedef struct micro_service_info_s microServiceInfo;
9509
9516typedef struct micro_service_stats_s microServiceStats;
9517
9518
9519extern NATS_EXTERN microError *micro_ErrorOutOfMemory;
9520extern NATS_EXTERN microError *micro_ErrorInvalidArg;
9521
// end of microTypes
9523
9542typedef microError *(*microRequestHandler)(microRequest *req);
9543
9566typedef void (*microErrorHandler)(microService *m, microEndpoint *ep, natsStatus s);
9567
9579typedef void (*microDoneHandler)(microService *m);
9580
// end of microCallbacks
9582
9593struct micro_endpoint_config_s
9594{
9600 const char *Name;
9601
9611 const char *Subject;
9612
9617 const char *QueueGroup;
9618
9623 bool NoQueueGroup;
9624
9629 natsMetadata Metadata;
9630
9634 microRequestHandler Handler;
9635
9640 void *State;
9641};
9642
9646struct micro_endpoint_info_s
9647{
9651 const char *Name;
9652
9656 const char *Subject;
9657
9662 const char *QueueGroup;
9663
9668 natsMetadata Metadata;
9669};
9670
9674struct micro_endpoint_stats_s
9675{
9676 const char *Name;
9677 const char *Subject;
9678
9683 const char *QueueGroup;
9684
9688 int64_t NumRequests;
9689
9694 int64_t NumErrors;
9695
9699 int64_t ProcessingTimeSeconds;
9700
9704 int64_t ProcessingTimeNanoseconds;
9705
9709 int64_t AverageProcessingTimeNanoseconds;
9710
9714 char LastErrorString[2048];
9715};
9716
9720struct micro_group_config_s
9721{
9723 const char *Prefix;
9724
9726 const char *QueueGroup;
9727
9729 bool NoQueueGroup;
9730};
9731
9738struct micro_service_config_s
9739{
9745 const char *Name;
9746
9750 const char *Version;
9751
9755 const char *Description;
9756
9761 const char *QueueGroup;
9762
9767 bool NoQueueGroup;
9768
9773 natsMetadata Metadata;
9774
9782 microEndpointConfig *Endpoint;
9783
9791 microRequestHandler StatsHandler;
9792
9800 microErrorHandler ErrHandler;
9801
9810 microDoneHandler DoneHandler;
9811
9819 void *State;
9820};
9821
9827struct micro_service_info_s
9828{
9832 const char *Type;
9833
9837 const char *Name;
9838
9842 const char *Version;
9843
9847 const char *Description;
9848
9852 const char *Id;
9853
9857 natsMetadata Metadata;
9858
9862 microEndpointInfo *Endpoints;
9863
9867 int EndpointsLen;
9868};
9869
9873struct micro_service_stats_s
9874{
9878 const char *Type;
9879
9883 const char *Name;
9884
9888 const char *Version;
9889
9893 const char *Id;
9894
9898 int64_t Started;
9899
9903 microEndpointStats *Endpoints;
9904
9908 int EndpointsLen;
9909};
9910
// end of microStructs
9912

Sets the NATS Options to use to create the connection.

Creates a stanConnOptions object.

Publishes data on a channel.

Creates a queue subscription.

Returns the message payload.

Returns the message's timestamp.
stanMsg_GetDataLength

Returns the message length.

Destroys the message object.

NATS_EXTERN void stanSubOptions_Destroy(stanSubOptions *opts)
Destroys a stanSubOptions object.
stanSubOptions_StartAtTimeDelta
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_StartAtTimeDelta(stanSubOptions *opts, int64_t delta)
Sets the desired start position based on the given delta.
stanSubOptions_SetMaxInflight
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_SetMaxInflight(stanSubOptions *opts, int maxInflight)
Sets the the maximum number of messages the cluster will send without an ACK.
stanSubOptions_StartAtSequence
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_StartAtSequence(stanSubOptions *opts, uint64_t seq)
Sets the desired start position based on the given sequence number.
stanSubOptions_SetManualAckMode

Sets the subscription's acknowledgment mode.
stanSubOptions_StartWithLastReceived
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_StartWithLastReceived(stanSubOptions *opts)
The subscription should start with the last message in the channel.
stanSubOptions_SetDurableName
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_SetDurableName(stanSubOptions *opts, const char *durableName)
Sets the Durable Name for this subscription.
stanSubOptions_StartAtTime
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_StartAtTime(stanSubOptions *opts, int64_t time)
Sets the desired start position based on the given time.
stanSubOptions_DeliverAllAvailable
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_DeliverAllAvailable(stanSubOptions *opts)
The subscription should start with the first message in the channel.
stanSubOptions_Create
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_Create(stanSubOptions **newOpts)
Creates a stanSubOptions object.
stanSubOptions_SetAckWait
NATS_EXTERN natsStatus stanSubOptions_SetAckWait(stanSubOptions *opts, int64_t wait)
Sets the timeout for waiting for an ACK from the cluster's point of view for delivered messages.

Returns the number of dropped messages.
